---
description: "Build a sleek, responsive one-page portfolio/resume website featuring hero section, skills, experience, projects, contact, and downloadable resume with modern styling and accessibility"
name: "Personal Portfolio/Resume Website"
argument-hint: "Share your name, profession, and 2-3 key projects or achievements to feature"
agent: "agent"
---

# Personal Portfolio/Resume Website

Create a professional, modern digital business card that showcases your skills, experience, and projects to potential employers or clients.

## 1. Project Structure & Setup

- **Choose stack**: HTML5 + CSS3 + vanilla JavaScript (simplest) or lightweight framework (React, Vue, or static site generator)
- **Initialize project**: Clean folder structure (src/, assets/, css/, js/)
- **Responsive framework** (optional): Bootstrap, Tailwind CSS, or custom CSS Grid/Flexbox
- **Version control**: Git repository with .gitignore, meaningful commits
- **Build tool** (optional): Vite or Webpack for asset bundling if using preprocessors

## 2. Hero Section

- **Eye-catching header**: Full-width or hero image with overlay
- **Professional introduction**: Name, title/role, compelling tagline (1-2 lines)
- **Call-to-action buttons**: "View My Work" or "Download Resume" prominent placement
- **Background**: Solid color, gradient, or subtle image/animation
- **Text contrast**: Ensure readability (WCAG compliance)

## 3. About/Profile Section

- **Short bio**: 2-3 sentences about yourself, background, professional goals
- **Profile image**: High-quality headshot or professional photo (optional but recommended)
- **Key highlights**: 2-3 bullet points of major achievements or specializations
- **Personal touch**: Genuine voice, authentic description
- **Visual balance**: Text and image layout that feels cohesive

## 4. Skills Section

- **Organize by category**: Technical skills, soft skills, tools, languages
- **Visual representation**: Skill badges, bars, or grid layout
- **Proficiency levels** (optional): Beginner, intermediate, advanced indicators
- **Prioritize**: Featured skills first, comprehensive list secondary
- **Keep updated**: Easy to modify for current proficiencies

## 5. Education Section

- **List degrees**: School name, degree type, graduation date
- **Include major/focus**: Relevant coursework or specialization
- **Certifications** (if applicable): Professional certifications, courses, bootcamps
- **GPA** (optional): Include if strong (3.5+) and relevant to role
- **Format**: Reverse chronological order (most recent first)

## 6. Work Experience Section

- **Job entries**: Company, title, dates employed, brief description
- **Bullet points**: 2-3 key responsibilities or achievements per role
- **Quantify impact** (if possible): Metrics, percentages, or measurable outcomes
- **Reverse chronological**: Most recent position first
- **Relevance**: Highlight roles most relevant to target career

## 7. Projects Section

- **Feature 3-5 key projects**: Personal, academic, or professional work
- **Project cards**: Title, description, technologies used, image/thumbnail
- **Links**: GitHub repository, live demo, or portfolio project page
- **Highlight diversity**: Show range of skills and interests
- **Include role**: What you specifically built or contributed

## 8. Contact Information

- **Primary contact method**: Email address prominently displayed
- **Alternative contact**: Phone (optional), physical location if applicable
- **Contact form** (optional): Simple form with Name, Email, Message fields
- **Clear visibility**: Easy to spot "Get in Touch" or "Contact" section
- **Email validation**: If form, basic client-side validation

## 9. Social Links & External Profiles

- **LinkedIn**: Professional networking profile link
- **GitHub**: Code portfolio and repository showcase
- **Twitter/X** (optional): If you share professional content
- **Portfolio platforms** (optional): Dribbble, Behance, CodePen if applicable
- **Visual treatment**: Icons with hover effects, grouped near footer or contact section
- **Open in new tab**: Links should open external sites appropriately

## 10. Downloadable Resume Button

- **Prominent placement**: Hero section or separate "Resume" button in navigation
- **File format**: PDF (best for compatibility)
- **Updated version**: Ensure resume matches portfolio content
- **Easy download**: One-click download, clear call-to-action
- **Fallback**: Link to hosted version if file download not available

## 11. Navigation & User Experience

- **Navigation bar**: Sticky header with smooth scroll to sections or fixed nav
- **Menu items**: Home, About, Skills, Experience, Projects, Contact, Resume
- **Mobile menu**: Hamburger menu for small screens, smooth collapse/expand
- **Scroll behavior**: Smooth scrolling between sections
- **Active state indicators**: Highlight current section in nav
- **Back-to-top button** (optional): For single-page navigation convenience

## 12. Modern Styling & Design

- **Color palette**: 2-3 primary colors + neutral grays, cohesive throughout
- **Typography**: 2-3 font families max (Google Fonts recommended)
- **Spacing & alignment**: Consistent padding, margins, visual hierarchy
- **White space**: Generous spacing to avoid clutter, breathable layout
- **Visual effects**: Subtle animations, hover states, transitions (not distracting)
- **Design consistency**: Cards, buttons, inputs styled uniformly

## 13. Mobile Responsiveness

- **Mobile-first approach**: Design for small screens first, scale up
- **Breakpoints**: Tablet (768px), desktop (1024px+) adjustments
- **Touch-friendly**: Button/link sizes ≥44x44px for mobile
- **Readable text**: Font sizes ≥16px on mobile, line-height ≥1.5
- **Test devices**: iPhone, Android tablet, desktop browser sizes
- **Performance**: Images optimized for mobile, lazy loading if needed

## 14. Accessibility Considerations

- **Semantic HTML**: Proper heading hierarchy (H1, H2, H3...), semantic tags
- **ARIA labels**: Form labels, icon descriptions, navigation landmarks
- **Color contrast**: WCAG AA compliance (4.5:1 for text, 3:1 for large text)
- **Keyboard navigation**: Tab through all interactive elements, visible focus indicators
- **Alt text**: Descriptive text for all images
- **Screen reader friendly**: Logical content order, skip navigation links
- **Motion**: Respect prefers-reduced-motion for animations

## 15. Code Quality & Documentation

- **Modular code**: Separate CSS files (styles, responsive), JS files (scroll, form)
- **Comments**: Explain complex sections, function purposes
- **Naming conventions**: Clear, descriptive class/ID names (BEM or consistent pattern)
- **Performance**: Minified CSS/JS, optimized images, lazy loading
- **README**: Setup instructions, deployment steps, browser compatibility list
- **Version control**: Meaningful commit messages, organized git history

## 16. Deployment & Distribution

- **Hosting platforms**: Netlify, GitHub Pages, Vercel (free tier, easy setup)
- **Custom domain** (optional): Point domain to hosted site
- **HTTPS**: Enable SSL certificate for security
- **Performance**: Lighthouse score target ≥80, mobile speed ≥Good
- **Analytics** (optional): Google Analytics or similar for traffic tracking
- **SEO basics**: Meta tags, title, description, open graph tags

## Output Requirements

- **Deliverables**: Source code, production build, README
- **Accessibility audit**: WCAG AA compliance checked
- **Performance metrics**: Lighthouse score, bundle size, load time
- **Browser compatibility**: Tested on Chrome, Firefox, Safari, mobile browsers
- **Code organization**: Clean project structure, modular components
- **Documentation**: Setup instructions, customization guide

**Style**: Professional, modern, beginner-friendly, portfolio-ready, production-quality
